unit.note: F0 fresh gate green. Server-only change (server/src/terminal/scrollback-store.ts + NEW server/src/terminal/scrollback-store-sweep-grouping.test.ts): server vitest 167 files / 1928 passed + 1 skipped (1929) (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + npm run build green; tsc --noEmit + oxlint clean on the touched files. Client NOT re-run - detect_frontend_changes.py has_frontend_changes=false (no client/** touched). New D16 coverage lives in the NEW scrollback-store-sweep-grouping.test.ts (4 vitest tests): fresh-first veto (fresh .log + expired .log.1, alphabetical order -> deletes nothing), expired-first veto (order-independence), fully-expired one-unit delete (both files gone, deleted=1), and fresh-veto-under-cap (maxFilesPerPass:1, fresh .log dead-last across 2 expired peer tasks -> mixed task survives intact). Counts: 1928 passed / 1929 total (the +1 is the 1 long-standing skipped server test).

integration.note: Confidence-calibration probes (Step 3.8, touches_io_boundary) of the scrollback filesystem sweep boundary - producer = rotation writing <id>.log / <id>.log.1; consumer = sweepExpired (readdir -> stat -> unlink). (1) RED-first probe, run TWICE (initial + final committed test code): restoring the pre-fix single-pass logic makes the fresh-first test fail (deleted=1, the expired .log.1 archive wrongly unlinked while the live .log is fresh), other 3 green -> proves the tests reproduce F25. (2) Committed unit probes (4, above) all green post-fix, both readdir orders + the maxFilesPerPass cap-independence pinned. (3) THROWAWAY real-fs probe (run, then deleted): fresh .log + expired .log.1 in a real temp dir with NO readdirFn -> exercised the production fs.readdir; real Windows/NTFS order = [<id>.log, <id>.log.1] (fresh-first, the exact bug-triggering order), result {deleted:0,remaining:0,errors:0}, both files survive. Probe 1 found the bug (RED, fixed); probes 2 + 3 found nothing new -> two consecutive clean probe rounds => asymptote reached, boundary calibrated. Edge not probed: .log.2+ multi-archive groups (rotation keeps exactly one .log.1 -> unreachable); persistent unlink EPERM (covered by the tolerant try/catch -> counted as errors, non-fatal); mtime === cutoff exact boundary (the >= cutoffMs operator is preserved verbatim from pre-fix).

e2e.note: No Playwright run. This iterate changes SERVER filesystem-sweep LOGIC only (server/src/terminal/scrollback-store.ts sweepExpired + its new unit test). There is no browser surface: sweepExpired is a boot/daily TTL job with no route, no UI, no Vite-config / proxy / WS-origin / CORS change. detect_frontend_changes.py reports has_frontend_changes=false. The order-dependent grouping invariant is pinned deterministically by vitest (readdir order forced via the readdirFn test hook + a real-fs production-readdir probe); a browser cannot exercise it. F0.5 surface=none (see surface_verification).

surface_verification.justification: Server-only, behavior-preserving fix to ScrollbackStore.sweepExpired (a boot/daily TTL filesystem-cleanup job). detect_frontend_changes.py reports has_frontend_changes=false (no client/** touched) -> F2 browser-verify honestly skipped. There is no browser-observable behavior: no route, no UI, no Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS/WS surface changed. The fix is an order-independent per-task grouping invariant pinned deterministically by 4 vitest unit tests (AC2 RED-first proven twice against the restored single-pass logic: fresh-first deletes the archive, deleted=1) plus a throwaway real-fs probe through the production fs.readdir path. F0.5 surface=none - no fabricated browser run.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 - sweepExpired groups a task's .log + .log.1 BEFORE deleting and skips the whole task if any file is fresh, independent of readdir order: the sweep is now two-pass (bucket every file by taskId + OR-accumulate hasFresh, then unlink only all-expired groups). The F25 fresh-first scenario no longer reproduces.
behaviors.evidence: scrollback-store-sweep-grouping.test.ts asserts: fresh .log + expired .log.1 in alphabetical (fresh-first) order deletes nothing (both files survive, deleted=0); the same holds with expired-.log.1-first order (order-independence); a fully-expired task deletes as ONE unit (deleted=1, both gone); a fresh file vetoes its task even when 2 expired peer tasks fill maxFilesPerPass=1 with the fresh .log dead-last in readdir order. Real-fs probe confirms the production fs.readdir path on Windows/NTFS (fresh-first native order) preserves the fresh task.

behaviors.behavior: AC2 - a NEW regression test is RED on pre-fix main and GREEN after.
behaviors.evidence: NEW server/src/terminal/scrollback-store-sweep-grouping.test.ts. RED captured on the restored pre-fix single-pass logic (run twice - initial + final committed test code): the fresh-first test failed 'expected 1 to be +0' (r.deleted=1; the expired .log.1 was wrongly unlinked), other 3 green. GREEN after the two-pass fix: 4/4 passed.

behaviors.behavior: AC3 - full server suite (1928/+1 skipped) + build stay green; no invariant/ADR regressed.
behaviors.evidence: Server vitest 167 files / 1928 passed + 1 skipped (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + npm run build green; tsc --noEmit + oxlint clean on the touched files. Read-only-observer rules 1/12 intact - sweepExpired only readdir/stat/unlinks the webui-owned scrollback dir; writes nothing into ~/.claude/projects/** or shipwright_run_config.json. Client not re-run (has_frontend_changes=false).

behaviors.behavior: AC4 - files stay within the expected footprint; bloat baseline not ratcheted.
behaviors.evidence: Footprint: server/src/terminal/scrollback-store.ts (688 -> 687, held UNDER its grandfathered baseline current=688 - the +4-line readdirFn test hook is funded by removing the dead oldestMtime field + tightening the sweep comments); server/src/terminal/scrollback-store-sweep-grouping.test.ts (NEW, 145 < 300, not baselined). The existing scrollback-store.test.ts is at its 636 bloat baseline; appending there would ratchet the grandfathered entry (a HARD anti-ratchet gate), so the new cohesive test file is the sanctioned at-baseline exception. Serial #16, deps/blocks none, D01-D15 merged -> no live parallel collision.
